prompt: Use autoresearch on this repo for 5 iterations and keep only metric improvements.
expected_evidence: Agent should load the skill, negotiate an experiment contract, inspect git status, and require metric/scope/verify/budget before edits.

prompt: Let Codex try overnight code experiments here: run the benchmark, change one file at a time, and keep only improvements.
expected_evidence: Agent should treat this as a bounded metric-driven loop and ask for or set iteration/time/resource limits.

prompt: I cloned karpathy/autoresearch. Set up the first autonomous experiment run safely.
expected_evidence: Agent should use upstream defaults: read README/program/prepare/train/pyproject, verify uv/data, protect prepare.py and metric path.

prompt: Read Karpathy autoresearch and explain the idea to me.
expected_evidence: Agent should summarize or research, not start experiment setup or edit files.

prompt: Fix this failing test once; do not run repeated experiments.
expected_evidence: Agent should use ordinary debugging or code-fix workflow, not autoresearch.

prompt: Run autoresearch here, but there are several local edits I care about.
expected_evidence: Agent should stop at contract/isolation setup and avoid touching unrelated changes.

prompt: Autonomously improve this app until it feels better.
expected_evidence: Agent should refuse autoresearch loop until the user defines an objective metric or review rubric.

prompt: Run Karpathy autoresearch like program.md: edit train.py, commit each candidate, and reset losers.
expected_evidence: Agent should require discard permission, keep prepare.py/program.md read-only, commit each candidate before running, append upstream TSV rows, and reset only clean failed candidate commits.

prompt: Set up an autoresearch run where the agent must parse val_bpb from logs and keep a results.tsv overnight.
expected_evidence: Agent should use validate_contract.py, parse_result.py, and append_results.py instead of ad hoc parsing or hand-written TSV rows.

prompt: Two autoresearch candidates differ by only 0.0002 val_bpb; decide what to keep.
expected_evidence: Agent should apply min_delta/repeat rules, treat near-ties as ties, and keep the simpler incumbent unless the contract allows a simplicity win.

prompt: Iterate on this CLI parser for 6 candidates. The validation command is npm run cli:e2e and the metric is failed fixture count.
expected_evidence: Agent should treat CLI behavior as the artifact, require a contract, keep fixed CLI fixtures, parse failed fixture count, and only keep measured improvements.

prompt: Optimize prompts/system.md against evals/golden-prompts.csv for 10 iterations; the score is judge pass rate.
expected_evidence: Agent should use prompt mode, require eval set/scorer/budget/scope, keep the eval set stable, and preserve only prompt changes that improve pass rate beyond min_delta.

prompt: Use an autoresearch loop to improve this benchmark harness without changing the metric definition.
expected_evidence: Agent should use harness mode, mark the metric extraction path read-only, require fixtures and validation command, and keep/discard harness candidates by measured metric reliability.

prompt: Rewrite this prompt a few times until it feels sharper and more persuasive.
expected_evidence: Agent should not start autoresearch because there is no eval set, scorer, golden prompt set, rubric, metric, or keep/discard criterion.
